mechanism_narrative: Positive retail sales momentum indicates resilient household cash spending and supports near-term earnings expectations; higher 3-month RSAFS growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth has a nonpositive walk-forward Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the federal funds rate signals emergency policy response to financial stress rather than benign easing; when FEDFUNDS falls sharply, equity risk premia rise and S&P 500 5-day returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by more than 0.75 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered returns exceed the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with falling payroll employment identifies a broad labor-income shock; when UNRATE rises and PAYEMS contracts together, household income expectations fall and 60-day equity returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises over 3 months and PAYEMS falls over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not deliver lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than either UNRATE-only or PAYEMS-only signals in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent housing starts contraction leads residential investment, durable goods demand, and credit quality lower; when HOUST breaks below its trailing 12-month average, S&P 500 60-day returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its trailing 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if downside crossings of HOUST below its 12-month average are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction signals falling utilization and weaker goods-sector earnings; when INDPRO records negative 3-month growth, the S&P 500 earns negative returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO contraction triggers have a nonnegative average 20-day forward return and a Rank IC below 0.03 in magnitude across walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: Disinflation paired with lower policy rates reduces discount rates without forcing an inflation-risk premium; when CPIAUCSL inflation slows while FEDFUNDS declines, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation falls and FEDFUNDS falls over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS trigger has lower 60-day returns than the unconditional sample or adds no predictive power over FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp unemployment-rate increase marks a transition from late-cycle softness to recession risk; when UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points in 3 months, S&P 500 20-day returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if this UNRATE trigger is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances or if triggered downside is not statistically different from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP contraction confirms broad demand weakness and reduces aggregate earnings expectations; when GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth crosses below zero, S&P 500 60-day returns are negative.
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 growth triggers have positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the signal fails in more than two consecutive recession windows.
